title: Electronegativity
short: A measure of how strongly an atom in a bond pulls shared electrons toward itself; larger values mean a stronger pull.
full:
Electronegativity describes the tendency of a bonded atom to attract the shared pair of electrons. Fluorine is the most electronegative element; values fall going down a group and rise going across a period.

The difference in electronegativity (ΔEN) between two bonded atoms predicts bond character: a very small difference gives a nonpolar covalent bond, a moderate difference (roughly 0.4–1.7) a polar covalent bond, and a large difference an ionic bond. Whether the whole molecule is polar also depends on its shape, because bond dipoles add as vectors.
